segments.title: ASW cruiser design trades firepower for screening utility
segments.summary: A UAD campaign stream turns a light-cruiser build into a practical sketch of an anti-submarine screening cruiser.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke uses the game design process to explain a specialist escort logic: speed, range, depth-charge capacity, reconnaissance, and fire-control value can matter more than heavy armament for an anti-submarine cruiser. The resulting ship is deliberately under-armed for a cruiser, but the tradeoff gives it a clear fleet-screening role around submarine hunting and long-range support.

evidence.note: The design is explicitly framed as an ASW cruiser with long-range anti-submarine and reconnaissance roles.
The ship is described as fast, under-armed, strong for submarine warfare, and fitted with many rangefinders.
